project.name: Afro Group — Cost Analysis Dashboard
project.description: End-to-end data pipeline: scrape cost data from Afro ERP → clean & categorise → interactive Streamlit dashboard

scraper.py.purpose: Selenium (Firefox) scraper — logs into ERP, selects all projects, downloads one CSV per expense category, returns combined DataFrame
scraper.py.public_api: scrape_cost_data(date_from, date_to, output_dir, username, password, access_code) -> pd.DataFrame
scraper.py.credential_resolution: parameter → env var (AFRO_USER / AFRO_PASS / AFRO_ACCESS) → hard-coded default
erp_nav_flow: 3. Select ALL projects, set date range, click Execute
4. Wait up to 120s for DataTable to render (TABLE_WAIT)
5. Switch page-length to 100 via JS injection
7. Open each href in a new tab via window.open()
8. Per tab: click CSV button, wait for new file, move to output_dir
decisions_and_fixes: TABLE_WAIT=120 separate from general WAIT_TIMEOUT=60 — ERP takes 60-90s to render results table
Collect hrefs BEFORE opening tabs to avoid StaleElementReferenceException
Use window.open() instead of .click() on link elements
_wait_for_new_csv() detects any new CSV in download folder (diff of before/after sets) — NOT hard-coded 'AFROerp.csv' because ERP changed filename format
ERP current filename format: 'Record of monthly Journal Entry for Settlement Category _CategoryName _..csv'
ERP old filename format: 'Record of monthly Journal Entry for Settlement Category _CategoryName __.csv'
Skip duplicate saves: if dest already exists → discard re-download, do not create _i copies
_load_csvs_to_dataframe deduplicates on (JE No., Project, JE Date, Debit, Credit) — keeps first occurrence
Dedup removed 1,621 duplicate rows in test run (3,469 raw → 1,848 unique)
'.' allowed in filename sanitisation so 'Dep. Installation Tools' stays intact

cleaner.py.purpose: Pandas cleaning pipeline — normalises raw ERP CSVs into analysis-ready DataFrame
pipeline_steps: 2. _extract_item         — regex on Source.Name filename to get expense item name
4. _parse_numeric        — removes commas, casts Debit/Credit to float, computes Cost amount = Debit - Credit
5. _map_category         — item → Category via CATEGORY_MAP; LG-number fallback → 'Finance Cost'
7. _compute_pct          — % = row Cost amount / total Cost amount

item_extraction_regex.primary: _(.+?) _[_\.]  — handles BOTH new (_.) and old (__) ERP filename formats
item_extraction_regex.fallback_1: _(.+?)__  — old double-underscore format without trailing space

known_unmapped_items_added_during_projec: Dep. laptop  → IT  (ERP uses lowercase 'l', map only had 'Dep.Laptop')
Dep_ Installation Tools → Tools  (scraper sanitised '.' to '_')

dashboard.py.purpose: Streamlit + Plotly interactive dashboard with 4-step sidebar workflow

step_1_credentials.note: Password required before Scrape button is enabled

options: Existing CSV folder — text input for path + Load & Clean button
Run scraper (live ERP) — date range, output folder, Clear checkbox + Scrape & Clean button
step_2_data_source.clear_before_scraping_checkbox: default ON — prevents duplicate accumulation across runs

step_3_cleaning.shows: row count, duplicate rows removed, unmapped category/account warnings

step_4_filters.note: All filters react together and update all KPIs and charts

chart_sections: Cost Breakdown: Cost by Account (horizontal bar, brand colours) + Cost by Category (donut)
Trends & Distribution: Monthly Cost by Account (stacked bar) + Account×Category Heatmap
Category & Item Detail: Category Cost by Account (stacked bar) + Top 15 Expense Items (table)
Account Share Analysis: % share bar per account
Project Drilldown: top 30 projects filterable by account (horizontal bar coloured by category)
Raw Data: expandable table + CSV download button
dashboard.py.session_state_key: df  — stores cleaned DataFrame so filters don't re-trigger load

data_pipeline.source_name_column: Source.Name — added by _load_csvs_to_dataframe, contains the CSV filename used for item extraction
data_pipeline.erp_filename_pattern: Record of monthly Journal Entry for Settlement Category _<ItemName> _..csv
data_pipeline.erp_filename_pattern_old: Record of monthly Journal Entry for Settlement Category _<ItemName> __.csv
data_pipeline.one_file_per: expense category (NOT per project) — each ERP tab = one category page

bugs_fixed_during_project.symptom: Scraper timed out waiting for results table

bugs_fixed_during_project.fix: Collect all hrefs first, then open with window.open()

bugs_fixed_during_project.bug: item = None on all scraped rows
bugs_fixed_during_project.symptom: Category = Unknown for all rows after scraping
bugs_fixed_during_project.root_cause: ERP changed filename suffix from '__' to '_.' — regex _(.+?)__ never matched
bugs_fixed_during_project.fix: Updated regex to _(.+?) _[_\.] which handles both formats

bugs_fixed_during_project.root_cause: CATEGORY_MAP had 'Dep.Laptop' (uppercase L) but ERP exports 'Dep. laptop' (lowercase, space)

bugs_fixed_during_project.symptom: 3469 rows instead of ~1184 after multiple scrape runs
bugs_fixed_during_project.root_cause: Browser re-downloads same CSVs on each scrape run, accumulating in output folder
bugs_fixed_during_project.fix: Three layers: (1) row-level dedup on JE No.+Project+JE Date+Debit+Credit; (2) scraper skips saving if file exists; (3) dashboard 'Clear folder before scraping' checkbox
Changed heat_df[...index] (column indexing) to heat_df.loc[...index] (row indexing)

bugs_fixed_during_project.symptom: Every download appeared to time out; files were saved by browser with ERP name
bugs_fixed_during_project.fix: Replaced _wait_for_file('AFROerp.csv') with _wait_for_new_csv() that detects any new CSV by set diff
Added format='%d %b %y' as primary parser (e.g. '31 Jan 26')

options.pros: Free, easiest, built for Streamlit, shareable URL
options.cons: Code must be on GitHub; ERP scraper won't work (no browser/Selenium); CSV upload flow works fine
options.best_for: Dashboard + CSV upload only (no live scraping)

options.platform: Docker on a VPS / company server
options.pros: Full pipeline works including Selenium scraper; team access via IP or domain
options.best_for: Full pipeline including live ERP scraping

options.cons: Cost; Selenium needs headless Firefox + geckodriver installed

pending_tasks.recommendation: If team only needs to view dashboards from CSV files → Streamlit Cloud. If team needs live ERP scraping → Docker on a VPS or company server.
pending_tasks.status: NOT YET DONE — user requested at end of conversation

conversation_decisions: Credentials (username, password, access_code) passed as parameters — never hard-coded in dashboard; sidebar inputs in Step 1
Cleaner runs automatically after every load/scrape — no separate 'Clean' button needed
Item filter added to sidebar (Step 4) alongside Account, Category, Month, Project
Project filter added to sidebar after user explicitly requested it
Dashboard uses dark chart theme (#0e1117 backgrounds) matching Streamlit dark mode
Cost amount = Debit - Credit (not JE net Amount) — more accurate for expense analysis
% computed from Cost amount total, NOT JE net Amount total
Unknown Category uses string 'Unknown', not NaN — easier to filter/count
LG-number items (Com LG XXXXXX) not in explicit CATEGORY_MAP fall back to 'Finance Cost'
Duplicate files: skip save (not _i suffix) to prevent dataset inflation
Dashboard shows dedup stats in Step 3 status message when duplicates are removed

how_to_resume_in_new_chat.instruction: Load this JSON file as context. The project is at E:/Claud code/Afro Autaomation/. Three files: scraper.py, cleaner.py, dashboard.py. Run: python -m streamlit run dashboard.py. The immediate next task is deployment — see pending_tasks section.
